message:
fix(manifest): improve cycle detection with proper DFS visiting/processed sets

The previous cycle detection used a single 'visited' set with backtracking
(delete after recursion), which works but is harder to reason about.

Refactored to use the standard DFS pattern with two separate sets:
- 'visiting': nodes currently in the recursion stack (back edges = cycles)
- 'processed': nodes fully explored (safe to skip)

This is clearer, matches standard graph algorithm implementations, and
correctly handles:
- Cross edges (visiting already-processed nodes)
- Back edges (detecting actual cycles)
- Forward edges (paths through already-processed subtrees)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
